findings.title: E2E badge-count seeding path is broken: wrong module path and wrong Dexie DB name
findings.why_it_matters: The test dynamically imports '/src/db.ts' which does not exist (actual path is '/src/db/index.ts' or '/src/db/schema.ts'). The catch fallback then opens Dexie with name 'knowlune', but the app's real DB is named 'ElearningDB' (see src/db/schema.ts:198). Both paths miss the real syncQueue table, so the badge assertion either passes for the wrong reason or never exercises refreshPendingCount. This false-positive test is worse than no coverage because it will not catch a regression in the badge display.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace dynamic import target with '/src/db/schema.ts' (matching nfr67 spec) and replace 'new Dexie("knowlune")' fallback with 'new Dexie("ElearningDB")'. Prefer reusing shared IndexedDB seeding helpers from tests/support/helpers/indexeddb-seed.ts to avoid this duplication per testing/test-patterns.md.

findings.title: Duplicate role=status and aria-live regions cause WAI-ARIA conflict and potential double announcements
findings.why_it_matters: The outer <button> carries role="status" and aria-live="polite" (lines 185-186), and nested inside it is a second <span role="status" aria-live="polite"> (line 210). This creates two live regions; the aria-label on the outer button already narrates status, and the inner span separately narrates transitions — which can lead to double or conflicting announcements. Additionally, role="status" on a button conflicts with the button's native role; screen readers may ignore one or the other unpredictably.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove role="status" and aria-live="polite" from the outer button (buttons are already focusable and announced via aria-label). Keep the inner sr-only live region as the single polite announcer for transitions into error/offline. Alternatively, render the live region as a sibling of the Popover, not inside the button, to avoid nested interactive/live-region semantics.

findings.title: Retry race-guard is insufficient: between getState() and fullSync() call, lifecycle may start another sync
findings.why_it_matters: handleRetry reads status via getState() (line 148), early-returns if syncing, then calls setStatus('syncing') and awaits fullSync(). But the periodic/online fullSync in useSyncLifecycle also calls setStatus('syncing') and invokes syncEngine.fullSync(). There is no mutex between the two — the guard only catches a status already set to syncing. If the 30s tick fires a nudge() + a reconnection fullSync between handleRetry's check and fullSync() call, two concurrent fullSync invocations race. Plan.md §'Retry flow' even admits 'Concurrent nudges are already serialized by syncEngine._runUploadCycle via navigator.locks' — but fullSync vs nudge serialization is not verified.
evidence: Plan notes lock-based serialization on uploads but not on fullSync cycles
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a module-level isFullSyncing flag in syncEngine with a single await-lock that both lifecycle and Retry share, or (b) change Retry to call a syncEngine.retry() wrapper that internally serializes against existing fullSync invocations. Add a unit test exercising concurrent lifecycle+Retry to lock in the guarantee.

findings.title: Reduced-motion test does not verify the static Cloud fallback — it only asserts absence of animate-spin
findings.why_it_matters: Plan.md §'Reduced motion' explicitly requires swapping to a static Cloud icon under prefers-reduced-motion (for WCAG 2.3.3 compliance). The test only asserts the class no longer contains 'animate-spin'. Since all four steady-state configs (synced/error/offline) also lack animate-spin, this test would pass even if the component rendered CheckCircle2 or Loader2-without-spin — neither of which is the specified Cloud fallback. Coverage gap for a stated WCAG requirement.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an assertion that the rendered icon element matches the Cloud lucide icon (e.g., via data-testid + parent class containing 'lucide-cloud' or by rendering StatusIcon with a stable display-name check). Cover the inverse: with reduced-motion NOT set, the Loader2 spinner is used.

findings.title: usePrefersReducedMotion duplicates logic that likely exists elsewhere in the codebase
findings.why_it_matters: A reduced-motion hook is typical shared infrastructure. Inlining it here creates drift risk across future components and duplicates matchMedia boilerplate. Not blocking, but worth extracting to src/app/hooks/usePrefersReducedMotion.ts or src/lib/hooks/ for reuse.
findings.suggested_fix: Extract usePrefersReducedMotion to a shared hook file and update imports. Check if other components (e.g., animations in figma/* or overview widgets) could adopt it to avoid future duplicate matchMedia handlers.

findings.title: Regex /\b5\d{2}\b/ could misclassify non-HTTP strings containing 3-digit numbers
findings.why_it_matters: A thrown error message like 'timed out after 500ms' or 'queue depth 503' would be misclassified as 'Server error'. Low-impact (user sees a benign user-safe bucket either way) but worth tightening if error messages from Dexie/Supabase ever surface such strings.
findings.suggested_fix: Tighten the regex to require HTTP-status context, e.g., /\b(?:status|http|code)\s*[:=]?\s*5\d{2}\b/i, or drop the message-based 5xx heuristic entirely and rely only on the numeric status/statusCode properties.

residual_risks: Working tree has uncommitted reverts of Unit 2 (useSyncLifecycle classifyError wiring + 30s refreshPendingCount) and Unit 4 (Layout.tsx mount of SyncStatusIndicator) including test assertions. The committed HEAD is complete; the uncommitted diff would regress R3 error-message classification and R5 passive-drift refresh if committed. Caller should decide whether to discard or commit those changes deliberately.
Retry concurrency with lifecycle fullSync not guarded by a shared lock (F3).
testing_gaps: No test covers the aria-live transition suppression rule (silent on synced<->syncing cycles, speaks only on transitions into error/offline).
Reduced-motion test does not assert the Cloud fallback (F4).
E2E badge-count test's seeding path is broken and does not verify real DB seeding (F1).
